ddls:
CREATE TABLE "algorithms" (
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each algorithm */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the algorithm */,
  "typical_code_framework" TEXT /* Typical code framework or implementation of the algorithm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the algorithm */,
  "order_of_growth" TEXT /* Order of growth (Big O notation) of the algorithm */,
  "example_use_case" TEXT /* Example use case or application of the algorithm */,
  "time_complexity_ratio" REAL /* Ratio of time complexity T(2N) / T(N) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the algorithm */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the algorithm */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the algorithm was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the algorithm */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the algorithm was last modified */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the algorithm (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("algorithm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm being measured */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., execution time, memory usage) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the performance metric (e.g., time, space) */,
  "recorded_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was recorded */,
  "run_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the performance was measured */,
  "input_size" INTEGER /* Size of the input data used for the performance measurement */,
  "run_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who ran the performance test */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the performance test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_run_by FOREIGN KEY ("run_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_algorithm_id FOREIGN KEY ("algorithm_id") REFERENCES algorithms ("algorithm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benchmarks" (
  "benchmark_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benchmark */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm being benchmarked */,
  "benchmark_name" TEXT /* Name of the benchmark */,
  "benchmark_value" REAL /* Value of the benchmark */,
  "benchmark_description" TEXT /* Description of the benchmark */,
  "recorded_date" TEXT /* Date when the benchmark was recorded */,
  "industry_standard" INTEGER /* Indicates if the benchmark is an industry standard */,
  "run_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the benchmark was recorded */,
  "input_size" INTEGER /* Size of the input data used for the benchmark */,
  "run_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who ran the benchmark test */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the benchmark test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benchmark_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmarks_algorithm_id FOREIGN KEY ("algorithm_id") REFERENCES algorithms ("algorithm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benchmarks_run_by FOREIGN KEY ("run_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, project manager, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the algorithm */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the algorithm was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_algorithm_id FOREIGN KEY ("algorithm_id") REFERENCES algorithms ("algorithm_id")
)
